title: Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary
tagline: Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Gujarat

overview.short: Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary is situated in the Banaskantha district formerly under Palanpur State in the Indian state of Gujarat at the Gujarat-Rajasthan border.
overview.description: Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary is situated in the Banaskantha district formerly under Palanpur State in the Indian state of Gujarat at the Gujarat-Rajasthan border. It was declared as a sanctuary in May 1978, covering an area of about 180 square kilometres (69 mi2), principally for protection of the sloth bear, which is now categorized as "Vulnerable A2cd+4cd;C1 ver 3.1" on the IUCN Red List. Their numbers are declining in the wild and they are threatened with extinction. The name "sloth" is said to be the epithet travellers and hunters in India gave to the bear when they saw it hanging upside d

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (129 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (134 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Jessore Sloth Bear Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
